title: digitalis lutescens
intro: Digitalis lutescens, also known as foxglove, is a biennial flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant contains cardiac glycosides, including digoxin and digitoxin, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various heart conditions, such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The medicinal properties of Digitalis lutescens have been shown to have several health benefits, including improving heart function, reducing inflammation, and lowering blood pressure. Digitalis lutescens is typically prepared as a tincture or extract, which can be used in herbal remedies, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it due to its potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
